quick_skim.title: Beyond Equidistant Assumptions: An Autoregressive Ordered Stereotype Model for Ordinal Time Series
quick_skim.abstract: We propose an extension of the ordered stereotype model (OSM) for ordinal time series data, referred to as the Autoregressive OSM (AR-OSM). The model captures serial dependence by incorporating lagged values of the response as covariates in the systematic component. In contrast to existing regression models for ordinal time series, the AR-OSM does not assume equidistant categories, but instead allows the data to determine their relative spacing. This property makes the model particularly suitable for applications where the equidistance assumption is unrealistic. Such a case is illustrated through the analysis of infant sleep state data. Additionally, a comprehensive simulation study is conducted to assess the performance of the model under varying sample sizes and to investigate how parameter values influence the induced serial dependence structure.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes an autoregressive model for ordinal sleep state time series
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出用于序数睡眠状态时间序列的自回归模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出用于序数睡眠状态时间序列的自回归模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出用于序数睡眠状态时间序列的自回归模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Autoregressive Ordered Stereotype Model (AR-OSM) for ordinal time series without equidistant assumption, demonstrated on infant sleep state data, enabling flexible measurement of sleep quality.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对序数时间序列数据提出自回归有序刻板模型（AR-OSM），该模型不要求类别等距，而是让数据决定类别间距。通过婴儿睡眠状态数据示例和全面的模拟研究，验证了模型在处理非等距睡眠状态测量中的有效性，为睡眠质量评估提供了更灵活可靠的统计方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对序数时间序列数据提出自回归有序刻板模型（AR-OSM），该模型不要求类别等距，而是让数据决定类别间距。通过婴儿睡眠状态数据示例和全面的模拟研究，验证了模型在处理非等距睡眠状态测量中的有效性，为睡眠质量评估提供了更灵活可靠的统计方法。
